# Why Teacher Development Programs Matter: How It Affects Student Success and Retention

![Writer: AMISA]()

Written By: [Ryan Stevenson](#viewer-1ir81), School Partnership Manager, [Teach Away](https://www.teachaway.com/)

Many schools prioritize professional development for teachers, and for good reason - it boosts their experience and encourages growth. But have you considered the impact a successful PD program can have on student performance and engagement?

The results of the NAEP's recent tests show that learning loss continues to be a problem during the pandemic. As a result, students need help finding the regular help they need to learn coupled with newer pandemic-related challenges.

Students need trained teachers. And teachers need the relevant training to have the [biggest chance](https://www.theedadvocate.org/the-significance-of-effective-teacher-training/) of succeeding in their profession.

## **The Significance Of Effective Teacher Training**

Teacher development is essential to a student's success. It provides teachers with the latest strategies and tactics and helps them navigate the challenges today's students face.

**What Are Teacher Development Programs (TDPs)?**

TDPs are professional development programs designed to continue a teacher's career development. PD can also be used to meet education initiatives, like the US Department of Education's Student Engagement [Initiative](https://www.ed.gov/news/press-releases/us-department-education-announces-engage-every-student-initiative-ensure-every-student-has-access-high-quality-learning), which aims to ensure every student has access to high-quality learning.

The [Teacher Development Program (TDP)](https://www.klassroom.com/pages/teacher-development-program) developed by Teach Away is an asynchronous online professional development program for international schools. TDP follows a U.S. framework, which places AMISA schools in a perfect position for the program. The coursework is based on an American curriculum and meets the Hawai’i and Arizona accreditation standards, allowing schools to upskill their staff with the current and best teaching practices in education.

With a flexible study plan, teachers are set-up for success through on-demand lessons in a user-friendly environment. Each week educators will submit assignments and reflections to an online grader who will provide professional feedback to enhance their learning.

TDP helps teachers develop their understanding of students on a deeper level with the program's comprehensive, research-based, and pedagogically-sound content.

## **Impact Of Teacher Development On Student Success**

TDPs ultimately affect the overall success of the students and the school through its impact on several critical factors. The positive impact a teacher's professional development has on their students is proven.

**Improved Student Performance**

[Research](https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/edlabs/regions/southwest/pdf/rel_2007033.pdf) shows that if teachers participate in a quality professional development program, they can raise their students' success rate by a considerable 21%. The same study shows that professional development affects student achievement by enhancing teacher knowledge and skills, improving classroom teaching, and raising student achievement.

**Increased Student Engagement**

A [study](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/21683603.2015.1064840?journalCode=usep20) designed to improve teachers' skills in creating personalized learning environments indicated that teachers who received special training reported higher levels of student engagement than students of teachers who had not.

Student engagement has many positive associations, including academic achievement and social-emotional well-being.

## **How Teacher Development Benefits Schools**

**Reduced Recruitment And Training Costs**

Quality professional development programs are a minimum requirement in [today's teacher market](https://www.teachaway.com/recruitment/blog/how-is-your-school-adapting-its-strategy-for-todays-teacher-market). Schools that can stand out from their competitors and offer teachers career growth save on future recruitment and training costs.

**Teacher Retention**

When teachers stay in their schools, the whole educational system benefits. Professional development programs like TDP attract and retain new teaching talent to your school and boost morale amongst your staff by supporting their development.

TDP is also a foundational building block for future teachers interested in becoming certified. Teach Away also offers a Teacher Certification Program, which goes a step further allowing teachers to obtain a US accredited teacher license post-qualification.

**Key Takeaways**

Studies show that Teacher Development Programs can impact student success by 21% and report higher student engagement than teachers who have not completed a TDP.

TDPs are an effective way for schools to adapt to today's teacher market while reducing recruitment and training costs.

TDPs attract and retain new teaching talent in your school.

Click to learn more about Teach Away's [Teacher Development Program](https://www.klassroom.com/pages/teacher-development-program).

**About Teach Away**

Teach Away offers solutions that provide innovative ways to license, recruit, and expand the impact of great teachers. Their customizable programs help create a healthy teacher pipeline at every level, including recruitment, teacher certification, and 1:1 online academic support with live teachers.

AMISA schools are a perfect fit for the TDP, for further information or to book a call, please contact Ryan Stevenson on [ryan@teachaway.com](mailto:ryan@teachaway.com).

---

With more enrolments last year coming from LATAM than any other region, Teach Away are looking forward to working with more AMISA schools in 2023, in helping to improve the development opportunities for local staff, and for schools to attract a more diverse and international workforce"*

The Impact of Teacher Professional Development on Student Success
=================================================================

![Image 2: teacher professional development](https://21stcented.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/thisisengineering-raeng-h6gCRTCxM7o-unsplash.jpg)

Students’ success largely depends on their teachers’ knowledge, skills, and motivation. As student needs continue to shift and learning styles change, educators need to be able to adapt and refine their practices to meet these changing needs. In pursuit of this adaptable nature, professional development is a source of educational improvement, refining teacher’s strategies and improving student success. Teacher professional development is a broad term that encompasses a variety of activities focused on enhancing educators’ knowledge, skills, aptitudes, and practices, ultimately driving student achievement. Join us as we explore the broad impact of teacher professional development on student success, notating strategies for improving teacher professional development.

Benefits of Teacher Professional Development for Student Success
----------------------------------------------------------------

Teacher professional development seeks to improve teacher practices and effectiveness. Professional development occurs through various ways, including seminars, online training, small groups, and mentorship programs. These sessions contribute to student success in a variety of ways. Here are some primary ways teacher professional development impacts teacher performance and student outcomes.

### Refining Pedagogical Strategies to Match Learning Styles

The primary purpose of professional development is to improve a teacher’s practices and pedagogical methods. The [methods by which an educator teaches can play a huge role in their students’ academic success](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9892052/). Professional development sessions and seminars can help teachers incorporate new research and methodologies into their teaching practices, allowing them to cater to students’ diverse needs and learning styles.

This not only helps teachers stay current with industry trends and research but also helps students achieve greater academic heights. As their teachers incorporate new teaching styles, students better understand their course content.

### Fostering Teacher Motivation and Confidence to Create Engaging Classrooms

Career development also serves as a morale booster for educators. These events and activities allow teachers to hone their skills and acquire new knowledge, making them feel more fulfilled and empowered.

These feelings of fulfillment and empowerment translate into the classroom, where students can experience their teachers’ newfound motivation and confidence. This creates a more engaging learning environment for students, encouraging them to participate more in their academic careers.

### Focusing on Student-Centered Learning

Teacher professional development can also help educators refocus their classrooms to include all students’ needs, aptitudes, and abilities. Through various educational and training seminars, educators can learn how to make academic experiences more personalized to individual students and their needs. This encourages students to take ownership of their learning and motivates them to be more engaged in their classrooms.

Ultimately, teacher professional development contributes to educators’ improvement and students’ long-term success.

Strategies for Enhancing Teacher PD and Maximizing Student Success
------------------------------------------------------------------

To meet the rapidly changing needs of learners, school boards and administrators must invest in quality teacher professional development. Several strategies help foster improvement and growth in educators’ skills and practices. Let’s look at a few critical strategies for enhancing teacher effectiveness and maximizing student success:

### Investing in Personalized and Sustained Professional Development Opportunities

All educators have different needs, interests, and styles. [Individualized professional development](https://21stcented.com/educator-feature/) plans help school districts meet these diverse needs and assist teachers in meeting their professional goals. Opportunities like self-directed learning, workshops, or coaching sessions with like-minded educators can provide teachers with new skills and practices to promote student achievement. Sustained professional development programs help teachers stay up-to-date on new research and methodologies for teaching, helping them further students’ education.

### Fostering Collaboration and Mentorship Among Educators

Mentorship among teachers can have an incredible impact on the quality and methodologies of novice and experienced teachers. Pairing these kinds of professionals together accelerates the professional development of early-career educators and helps experienced educators gain more practical experience.

### Utilizing Technology for Accessible and Scalable Professional Development Solutions

The integration of technology into teacher professional development holds much promise. Online learning platforms and digital tools provide educators access to numerous development resources like virtual training sessions, webinars, self-paced courses, and discussion forums. These resources allow teachers to engage in learning activities at their convenience and enable them to connect with colleagues regardless of geographic location.

By implementing these strategies, school boards and administrators can create a culture of continuous learning and improvement. This fosters teacher growth and student success.

Measuring the Impact of Teacher Professional Development
--------------------------------------------------------

When designing a teacher professional development program to improve student success, school administrators must have several key performance indicators (KPIs) in place to measure the program’s success. Here are some indicators to consider:

### Academic Achievement

Studies clearly show a correlation between [sustained teacher professional development and student achievement](https://files.eric.ed.gov/fulltext/ED498548.pdf). Choosing several key academic metrics, like term GPA and percentage of failed courses, can help administrators monitor the success of teacher professional development programs.

### Graduation Rate

Monitoring graduation rates over several years can also reveal information about the long-term effects of professional development. Increasing graduation rates may positively impact student success.

### Student Engagement

As mentioned, teacher professional development helps students engage in their classrooms and learning. By monitoring how students interact with the material they are studying, administrators and educators can gain information about the effectiveness of professional development initiatives.

Maximize Teacher Professional Development
-----------------------------------------

Are you ready to unlock the full potential of this process and transform student success in your school environment? Look no further than 21st Century Ed. With a comprehensive suite of solutions designed to meet the diverse needs of school districts, 21st Century Ed offers a range of tools and resources to support administrators and educators in guiding students to academic excellence.
